title: Doubling down on open-access quantum computing | IBM Quantum Computing Blog
why:
- IBM is expanding its Quantum Open Plan by offering new learning resources and increased free access to advanced quantum hardware.
- Users who log 20 minutes of quantum compute in a 12‑month period can opt into a special promotion for 180 minutes of runtime over the next 12 months, a significant increase from the standard 10 minutes per month.
- The IBM Quantum Heron r2 ibm_kingston, one of IBM’s highest-performing quantum computers, is now available to all Open Plan users, enabling access to up to 340k circuit layer operations per second with improved error rates.
- Even with the standard 10 minutes of runtime, users can run most Qiskit tutorials and experiment with sophisticated quantum techniques; the 180-minute allotment allows for advanced research, including the ability to recreate landmark experiments demonstrating quantum utility.
- New educational offerings, such as the "Designing and leading quantum projects" course, are intended to help users plan quantum initiatives and write grants to continue research after exhausting their free runtime allocation.
